reasoning: Each block requests 24,576 × 4 = 96 KB of dynamic shared memory, which on an A10G (Ampere, 100 KB configurable shared memory per SM) allows only 1 block per SM. With 128 threads per block, that yields only 4 warps per SM out of a maximum of 48, giving ~8.3% occupancy — far below the 35% threshold. While the inner loop has high arithmetic intensity (12 FLOPs × 64 repeats × 96 iters per 2 global loads per outer iteration puts it well above the ridge point in theory), the extremely low occupancy means the SM cannot hide instruction latencies or memory latencies, leaving execution units idle. The massive shared memory footprint is the root cause, throttling occupancy to a single block per SM and making the kernel unable to saturate compute pipelines.
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: occupancy-limited
CONFIDENCE: HIGH

With 128 threads per block, that yields only 4 warps per SM out of a maximum of 48, giving ~8.3% occupancy — far below the 35% threshold. While the inner loop has high arithmetic intensity (12 FLOPs × 64 repeats × 96 iters per 2 global loads per outer iteration puts it well above the ridge point in theory), the extremely low occupancy means the SM cannot hide instruction latencies or memory latencies, leaving execution units idle. The massive shared memory footprint is the root cause, throttling occupancy to a single block per SM and making the kernel unable to saturate compute pipelines.

prompt.system: You are a GPU performance engineer. Follow the requested format exactly.

judge.explanation: The response correctly identifies the occupancy-limited bottleneck, cites specific metric values (8.3% occupancy, 96 KB shared memory, 1 block/SM, 4 warps/SM), correctly attributes the root cause to shared memory footprint throttling occupancy rather than primarily blaming inner-loop arithmetic intensity, and HIGH confidence is appropriate given the correct diagnosis.
